title: Hamas 'Considering' Board of Peace Disarmament Proposal; Response Pending
detail: As of March 28, 2026, Hamas confirmed it is formally reviewing the Board of Peace disarmament framework presented by UN Gaza High Representative Nickolay Mladenov. Mediators indicated a Hamas response is expected 'in coming days or weeks.' The plan calls for an eight-month phased decommissioning of all offensive weapons in exchange for international reconstruction funding and a staged Israeli military withdrawal. Hamas sought to distinguish between heavy weapons (rockets, mortars) and light arms (rifles), signaling partial rather than full compliance. Senior Hamas official Bassem Naim accused Mladenov of 'siding with Israel' by conditioning reconstruction on disarmament. Palestinian Islamic Jihad and two other factions issued statements rejecting the framework outright.

title: IDF Drone Strike Kills One in Khan Younis; Disputed Shejaiya Deaths
detail: Palestinian media reported one person killed in an IDF drone strike in Khan Younis on March 28, 2026, during the ongoing ceasefire. Separately, two brothers were killed in the Shejaiya neighborhood of Gaza City. The IDF stated it had no active operations in the Shejaiya area at the time and suggested the deaths may have resulted from a Palestinian-on-Palestinian armed altercation. Gaza ceasefire monitoring groups counted this among thousands of alleged Israeli violations since October 10, 2025. As of mid-March 2026, the Gaza Government Media Office had documented at least 2,073 Israeli ceasefire violations resulting in 713 Palestinian deaths since the ceasefire's start.

title: Three Journalists Killed in Israeli Strike on Press Vehicle in Lebanon
detail: An Israeli airstrike on March 28, 2026 struck a clearly marked press car near Jezzine in southern Lebanon, killing three journalists: Ali Shoaib (Al-Manar TV), Fatima Ftouni (Al-Mayadeen TV), and Mohamad Ftouni (freelance). The CPJ opened an investigation. The strike occurred amid the broader Israel-Lebanon war that resumed in early March 2026 following Israeli strikes on Iranian nuclear sites. Israel had already surpassed all other governments in journalist killings since CPJ began tracking in 1992, with at least 259 confirmed journalist deaths in the Gaza conflict alone.
